text: I visit this Bharat Petroleum CNG & Petrol Pump every morning. Unfortunately, the drinking water purifier is almost always not working and there is no drinking water available. Many people come here daily and feel thirsty. Request the management to please fix the drinking water facility as soon as possible.

text:
Decent petrol pump with huge rush for CNG and comparatively less rush for petrol/diesel.
They also provide Speed petrol but there is only 1 counter in the last lane before CNG.

station_name: Bharat Petroleum, Petrol Pump - OM Namah Petroleum
station_address: Cts No. 1820, 1821, Adinath Marg, Off Link Rd, near Aura Hotel Junction, Yogi Nagar, Borivali, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400091
text:
🚨 Beware of Cheating at this Petrol Pump! 🚨

I had a very bad experience here with DEF fluid filling. First of all, when I asked one person if DEF was available, he confidently said “No”. Then another person came and said “Yes” – clearly trying to confuse customers.

I purchased 10 liters DEF for ₹850. My car’s DEF tank capacity is 20 liters, and it was already at 54% (Approximately 10 litres). So, after adding 10 liters, it should have reached 100% full.

But after they “filled” it, the meter showed only 71%. That means they filled just 4 liters instead of 10 liters. When I questioned them, they gave silly excuses like “The remaining fluid in the can won’t come out”. They also did not even use a proper funnel – just pushed a pipe inside carelessly.

When I checked the can, more than 5 liters were still left inside, but the staff shamelessly claimed it was “finished”. I’ve also added pictures of Car DEF level and remaining volume of DEF in Can. This is nothing but open cheating and fraud with customers.

1)  They charge for 10 liters but fill less.
2)  Staff keep confusing answers to trap customers.
3)  No proper equipment or honesty in service.

⚠️ My advice: Avoid this petrol pump at all costs. They are looting people in the name of DEF fluid. Please always come out of your vehicle and check carefully while refueling here. This pump cannot be trusted.

Details for Reference (if required):
• Vehicle No.: MH47BE0360
• Date & Time of Purchase: 29 August 2025, ~7:30 PM

station_name: Bharat Petroleum, Petrol Pump - OM Namah Petroleum
station_address: Cts No. 1820, 1821, Adinath Marg, Off Link Rd, near Aura Hotel Junction, Yogi Nagar, Borivali, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400091
text:
Quick service, decent staff aswell with Speed petrol available. I have been exclusively filling up Speed petrol but needed to go to the one opposite Borivali railway station but this is my go to. There is only one machine that has Speed petrol and normal petrol and is converted to a 2 wheeler lane so you need to take your car at that blocked off lane at that machine and it increases the wait time as they generally work to clear out the 2 wheelers first even though there is a functional machine right in front of this machine that remains free. This needs to be addreses by either changing the location of that machine or adding another one. Also have had times when speed petrol was unavailable, good to have that in mind aswell.
Overall a great experience for the customer with the staff and quality of fuel and in addition to that the wait times are less !

text: I filled my tank full in my bike in 19th Aug 2025. Suddenly the next day it refused to start. Had to remove the whole petrol. Filled 5 litres from that in my car again, car started misbehaving too. I used to fill petrol regularly from here. But now onwards I'll be careful not to. Might be the rain water that have mixed in their underground tank.
